title: Quinn Jurvan
content:
Quinn Jurvan led a research group at the Dorsaly Field Research Campus in Lunaris that advanced subsurface acoustic tomography. The group's foundational results were published in 1840.

Theory.

Commercial licenses emerged after 2011, though academic use remains governed by a shared patent pool.

Apparatus.

Graduate instrumentation courses now teach the principles behind the 1840 measurement protocol.

Methods.

Subsequent revisions added adaptive filtering that reduced noise during long-exposure runs.

Results.

The laboratory released open calibration datasets that other sites adopted within months.

Applications.

Applications include structural monitoring of bridges and precision alignment in turbine manufacturing.

Criticism.

Annual conferences in Lunaris feature workshops devoted to safety protocols for field deployment.

Theory.

The method relies on low-temperature chambers calibrated against reference beams from a sealed interferometer ring.

Apparatus.

Pilot studies in 1838 demonstrated stability improvements over earlier thermal imaging pipelines.

Methods.

Peer review highlighted reproducibility across three independent sites, including a winter trial in Lunaris.

Results.

Criticism focused on sampling bias when instruments were operated without controlled humidity shields.

Applications.

Commercial licenses emerged after 2011, though academic use remains governed by a shared patent pool.

Criticism.

Graduate instrumentation courses now teach the principles behind the 1840 measurement protocol.

Theory.
